reasoning: The current observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, and total_distance=60. Since 10+20+30=60, it appears the equation is total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3. To confirm this hypothesis, I will change one variable (increase d1) and observe if total_distance increases by the same amount, keeping d2 and d3 constant.

The previous step increased d1 from 10 to 20, and total_distance increased from 60 to 70. This confirms a linear relationship where total_distance changes by the same amount as d1. The initial state (d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, total=60) also fits total = d1 + d2 + d3 (10+20+30=60). The current state (d1=20, d2=20, d3=30, total=70) fits 20+20+30=70. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation is total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3.
